description: Three-way performance comparison: will Gold outperform both Bitcoin and the S&P 500 in 2026? Currently the favorite at 54%, reflecting risk-off macro sentiment and strong YTD gold performance.
question: Will Gold have the best performance in 2026 (vs Bitcoin and S&P 500)?

resolution_logic.summary: Resolves Yes if Gold's percentage price change in 2026 exceeds both Bitcoin's and S&P 500's. Uses MarketWatch Gold Continuous Contract (GC00) close prices for last trading day of 2025 vs last trading day of 2026.

triggers.description: Gold YTD return exceeds both Bitcoin and S&P 500 YTD returns
triggers.metric: Gold Continuous Contract (GC00) % change (MarketWatch close, last trading day 2025 vs last trading day 2026)
triggers.threshold: Greater than both BTC (Binance) and S&P 500 (Yahoo Finance)

exclusions: Total return measures — only closing prices
Spot gold prices from other sources — must be GC00 continuous contract on MarketWatch
resolution_logic.ambiguity_notes: Gold uses futures continuous contract, not spot — these can diverge due to contango/backwardation. 'Last trading day' is typically December 31 but could be December 30 if Dec 31 falls on a weekend or holiday. MarketWatch historical data is the authoritative source.

catalyst_dates.event: April FOMC — rate decisions drive gold via real yield channel
June FOMC + dot plot — forward guidance moves gold
December FOMC — last major catalyst before year-end

cross_platform_link.structural_notes: No equivalent on Kalshi. Coverage gap.

related_markets.notes: Bitcoin best performer at 30% — Gold is the current favorite at 54%, reflecting risk-off macro sentiment
S&P 500 best performer at 20% — equities lagging in this race
